text:
Once upon a time, there was an old woman who loved birds. She lived in the heart of a big city. Everyday, she would take long walks in the park, just listening to their melodious songs. One day, she noticed a young bird learning to fly. Every time my favorite bird would fall back down, the old woman would gently shout encouragement. Eventually, my bird masterbird fluttered beautifully through the air. Every time I saw my old lady bird flying strong, I knew my own dreams were soaring. And every springtime song I heard, I just sang back to our city bird.

That’s all I want—I just want to sing.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
